title: permissions.enabled opt-in means OC is allow-all by default — diverges from CC always-on model
description:
## Summary

OC's PermissionsConfig (src/config/permissions.rs:10) defaults enabled to false via #[serde(default)]. When enabled=false, PermissionManager::check() short-circuits to CheckResult::Allowed for every call (src/permissions.rs:132-134).

CC has no equivalent gate — the permission pipeline always runs. A CC deployment always checks permissions. An OC deployment that omits permissions.enabled: true from config silently allows all tool calls, including rm -rf / and writes to .git/config.

## Security relevance (high)
This is a default-open vs default-closed security posture difference. OC ships as default-open; CC ships as default-closed. A fresh OC deployment is functionally equivalent to CC's bypassPermissions mode, but without any audit trail or user awareness.

## Behaviors to document/gate
- Ensure the enabled=false default is explicit in documentation and CLI warnings
- Consider whether enabled should default to true for production use
- Add test asserting that enabled=false allows rm -rf / (to document the intentional default)
- Add test asserting that enabled=true + empty default_allow denies rm -rf / (deny-by-default)

## Parent issue
#531

## References
- CC: utils/permissions/permissions.ts:1158-1319 (always-on pipeline)
- OC: src/config/permissions.rs:10-11
- OC: src/permissions.rs:132-134
